author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XIV. 4–6.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have now the opportunity, dearly, beloved, as far as we can, of understanding the earlier words of the Lord from the later, and His previous statements by those that follow, in what you have heard was His answer to the question of the Apostle Thomas.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the Lord was speaking above of the mansions, of which He both said that they already were in His Father’s house, and that He was going to prepare them; where we understood that those mansions already existed in predestination, and are also being prepared through the purifying by faith of the hearts of those who are hereafter to inhabit them, seeing that they themselves are the very house of God; and what else is it to dwell in God’s house than to be in the number of His people, since His people are at the same time in God, and God in them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To make this preparation the Lord departed, that by believing in Him, though no longer visible, the mansion, whose outward form is always hid in the future, may now by faith be prepared: for this reason, therefore, He had said, “And if I go away and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you to myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In reply to this, “Thomas saith unto Him, Lord, we know not whither Thou goest: and how can we know the way?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Both of these the Lord had said that they knew; both of them this other declares that he does not know, to wit, the place to which, and the way whereby, He is going.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he does not know that he is speaking falsely; they knew, therefore, and did not know that they knew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He will convince them that they already know what they imagine themselves still to be ignorant of.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, and the truth, and the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, brethren, does He mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, we have just heard the disciple asking, and the Master instructing, and we do not yet, even after His voice has sounded in our ears, apprehend the thought that lies hid in His words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is it we cannot apprehend?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Could His apostles, with whom He was talking, have said to Him, We do not know Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, if they knew Him, and He Himself is the way, they knew the way; if they knew Him who is Himself the truth, they knew the truth; if they knew Him who is also the life, they knew the life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, you see, they were convinced that they knew what they knew not that they knew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it, then, that we also have not apprehended in this discourse?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What else, think you, brethren, but just that He said, “And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And here we have discovered that they knew the way, because they knew Him who is the way: the way is that by which we go; but is the way the place also to which we go?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet each of these He said that they knew, both whither He was going, and the way.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There was need, therefore, for His saying, “I am the way,” in order to show those who knew Him that they knew the way, which they thought themselves ignorant of; but what need was there for His saying, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life,” when, after knowing the way by which He went, they had still to learn whither He was going, but just because it was to the truth and to the life He was going?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By Himself, therefore, He was going to Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whither go we, but to Him, and by what way go we, but by Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, therefore, went to Himself by Himself, and we by Him to Him; yea, likewise both He and we go thus to the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He says also in another place of Himself, “I go to the Father;” and here on our account He says, “No man cometh unto the Father but by me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And in this way, He goeth by Himself both to Himself and to the Father, and we by Him both to Him and to the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who can apprehend such things save he who has spiritual discernment? and how much is it that even he can apprehend, although thus spiritually discerning?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Brethren, how can you desire me to explain such things to you?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Only reflect how lofty they are.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see what I am, I see what you are; in all of us the body, which is corrupted, burdens the soul, and the earthly tabernacle weigheth down the mind that museth upon many things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do we think we can say, “To Thee have I lifted up my soul, O Thou that dwellest in the heavens”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But burdened as we are with so great a weight, under which we groan, how shall I lift up my soul unless He lift it with me who laid His own down for me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I shall speak then as I can, and let each of you who is able receive it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As He gives, I speak; as He gives, the receiver receiveth; and as He giveth, there is faith for him who cannot yet receive with understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, saith the prophet, “If ye will not believe, ye shall not understand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Tell me, O my Lord, what to say to Thy servants, my fellow-servants.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Apostle Thomas had Thee before him in order to ask Thee questions, and yet could not understand Thee unless he had Thee within him; I ask Thee because I know that Thou art over me; and I ask, seeking, as far as I can, to let my soul diffuse itself in that same region over me where I may listen to Thee, who usest no external sound to convey Thy teaching.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Tell me, I pray, how it is that Thou goest to Thyself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Didst Thou formerly leave Thyself to come to us, especially as Thou camest not of Thyself, but the Father sent Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I know, indeed, that Thou didst empty Thyself; but in taking the form of a servant, it was neither that Thou didst lay down the form of God as something to return to, or that Thou lost it as something to be recovered; and yet Thou didst come, and didst place Thyself not only before the carnal eyes, but even in the very hands of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how otherwise save in Thy flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By means of this Thou didst come, yet abiding where Thou wast; by this means Thou didst return, without leaving the place to which Thou hadst come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, by such means Thou didst come and return, by such means doubtless Thou art not only the way for us to come unto Thee, but wast the way also for Thyself to come and to return.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when Thou didst return to the life, which Thou art Thyself, then of a truth that same flesh of Thine Thou didst bring from death unto life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word of God, indeed, is one thing, and man another; but the Word was made flesh, or became man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so the person of the Word is not different from that of the man, seeing that Christ is both in one person; and in this way, just as when His flesh died.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ died, and when His flesh was buried, Christ was buried (for thus with the heart we believe unto righteousness, and thus with the mouth do we make confession unto salvation); so when the flesh came from death unto life, Christ came to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because Christ is the Word of God, He is also the life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thus in a wonderful and ineffable manner He, who never laid down or lost Himself, came to Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But God, as was said, had come through the flesh to men, the truth to liars; for God is true, and every man a liar.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, He withdrew His flesh from amongst men, and carried it up there where no liar is found, He also Himself—for the Word was made flesh—returned by Himself, that is, by His flesh, to the truth, which is none other but Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this truth, we cannot doubt, although found amongst liars, He preserved even in death; for Christ was once dead, but never false.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Take an example, very different in character and wholly inadequate, yet in some lit tle measure helpful to the understanding of God, from things that are in peculiarly intimate subjection to God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See here in my own case, while as far as pertains to my mind I am just the same as yourselves, if I keep silence I am so to myself; but if I speak to you something suited to your understanding, in a certain sense I go forth to you without leaving myself, but at the same time approach you and yet quit not the place from which I proceed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when I cease speaking, I return in a kind of way to myself, and in a kind of way I remain with you, if you retain what you have heard in the discourse I am delivering.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if the mere image that God made is capable of this, what may not God, the very image of God, not made by, but born of God; whose body, wherein He came forth to us and returned from us, has not ceased to be, like the sound of my voice, but abides there, where it shall die no more, and death shall have no more dominion over it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Much more, perhaps, might and ought to have been said on these words of the Gospel; but your souls ought not to be burdened with spiritual food, however pleasant, especially as the spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak.
